ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'white wine' class (FOODON_03301699); no specific Grillo varietal entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code GY1ER represents white wine generically. No Grillo-specific or Terre Siciliane IGT code exists in FoodEx2 catalogue; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 53410008 'Wine (substance)' is the closest defensible ancestor; no white wine or Grillo varietal-specific SNOMED code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of white wine is approximately 148 g (approximately 150 ml), equivalent to a single restaurant pour.

nutrient_source.title: Wine, table, white — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174837)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for generic white table wine used as the closest approved source. Grillo is a dry Sicilian white wine with a nutritional profile consistent with generic dry white wine. Values adapted from USDA FDC ID 174837. Alcohol content of Grillo is typically 12–13% ABV, consistent with the USDA reference entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists specifically for Grillo DOC wine. Dry white wine has a GI of approximately 45 per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283), which lists white wine at GI ~45. Grillo is a dry white wine with low residual sugar (~2.6 g carbohydrate per 100 ml), consistent with this reference. GL per serving calculated as: (45 × 2.6 × 1.48) / 100 = 1.73, rounded to 1.8.

hypertension.guidance: Alcohol consumption, even in moderate amounts, can raise blood pressure over time; individuals with hypertension should limit wine intake to no more than one standard drink per day and monitor blood pressure response.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol and low but present residual carbohydrate
type2_diabetes.guidance: Dry white wine such as Grillo has a low GI (~45) and low residual sugar (~2.6 g per 100 ml), but alcohol can cause hypoglycaemia in individuals on insulin or sulfonylureas; people with Type 2 Diabetes should consume wine only with food and in moderation, not exceeding one standard drink per occasion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Alcohol-associated cardiovascular risk at higher intake levels
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While light-to-moderate wine consumption has been associated with modest cardiovascular benefit in observational studies, current clinical guidelines do not recommend initiating alcohol consumption for cardiovascular protection; individuals with established cardiovascular disease should limit intake to one standard drink per day or less, consistent with national heart foundation recommendations.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grillo DOC White Wine, Terre Siciliane IGT, Assuli, 2016 is a Sicilian dry white wine produced from the Grillo grape variety, an indigenous Sicilian cultivar historically used in Marsala production but increasingly vinified as a varietal dry white wine. The Assuli estate is located in western Sicily, and the 2016 vintage was produced under the Terre Siciliane IGT (Indicazione Geografica Tipica) designation. The wine presents a bright straw-yellow colour with greenish reflections and a wide, fruity aromatic bouquet featuring notes of tropical fruit including pineapple and citron, along with floral and mineral undertones characteristic of the Grillo variety. On the palate it is dry, fresh, and medium-bodied with lively acidity and a clean, persistent finish. Nutritionally, dry white wine such as Grillo contains approximately 82 kcal per 100 ml, with minimal protein (0.07 g), negligible fat (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central), and low residual carbohydrate (2.6 g per 100 ml, primarily as residual sugars). The primary caloric contributor is ethanol. The glycemic index of dry white wine is approximately 45 (low GI), with a glycemic load of approximately 1.8 per standard 148 ml serving. Clinically, alcohol in wine can raise blood pressure, interact with diabetes medications, and contribute to cardiovascular risk at higher intake levels. Sulphites are present as a declared allergen. This wine is served at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk in Singapore and is priced at SGD 97.19 per bottle on field survey data.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy generic white table wine (FDC ID 174837), which is an approved secondary source. No Grillo-specific or Assuli-branded nutritional analysis was available in any approved primary source. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for dry white wine. Confidence is medium because the match is to a generic white wine category rather than this specific product.
notes: 1. fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for white table wine; naturally zero by product composition as wine contains no fat. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are all confirmed zero per the same source. 2. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; naturally zero as wine contains no dietary fibre. 3. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; naturally zero as wine is a plant-derived fermented beverage. 4. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_e_mg are confirmed zero or effectively zero per USDA FoodData Central for white wine. 5. The input food_group was listed as 'Grains and staples', which is incorrect for a wine; corrected to 'Beverages'. 6. The cuisine_tags in the input (Pasta, Pizza, Chicken Dish) are restaurant category tags from field survey and do not describe this product; they have been excluded from the output. 7. glycemic_index set to 45 (low GI) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 for dry white wine; gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55). 8. is_vegan set to false because many wines use animal-derived fining agents (e.g., isinglass, casein, egg albumin); Assuli fining practice is not confirmed from an approved source. 9. All -1 sentinel values in per_100g have been replaced with actual USDA-reported values where available. No -1 values remain in this record.
